rulings.Case Title: VERNON LEROY GIESICK et al VS. ACANDS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MARCH 14, 2008 LINE 1. DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. DECEDENT IDENTIFIED A DISCRETE NUMBER OF EMPLOYERS. NOT ONLY DID DEFENDANT FAIL TO ADDRESS WHAT THESE WITNESSES KNEW, ONE, MR. SILVERA HAD GIVEN A DECLARATION PLACING DEFENDANT'S PRODUCT AT THE WORKSITE. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED ON PUNITVE DAMAGES BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS WAIVED THE CLAIM; IT IS GRANTED AS TO BREACH OF WARRANTY FOR THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT DECEDENT OR PLAINTIFF PURCHASED DEFENDANT'S PRODUCT. DENIED AS TO NEGLIGENCE AND PRODUCT LIABILITY CLAIMS. ASBESTOS IS AN INHERENTLY DANGEROUS PRODUCT AND THERE IS EVIDENCE OF DECEDENT'S EXPOSURE TO IT. AS TO THE FRAUD AND CONSPIRACY CLAIM, THERE NEEDS TO BE A HEARING ON THE ADMISSIBILITY OF PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JUDITH CHOW et al VS. THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MARCH 14, 2008 LINE 4. DEFENDANTS THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA, ROBERT HAWLEY, ELYSE CONTANT, STARR BABCOCK AND PEGGY VAN HORN'S Motion For Summary Adjudication IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF IN THAT DEFENDANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT PLAINTIFF IS UNABLE TO PROVE HER CLAIM OF RETALIATION. PLAINTIFF IS COMPLAINING ABOUT MULTIPLE ACTS OF RETALIATION AND THE COMPLAINT AT PARAGRAPH THIRTY-TWO IS SUFFICIENT TO PLACE DEFENDANT ON NOTICE OF THIS CLAIM AS WAS THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF PLAINTIFF. UNDER MCRAE V DEPARTMENT OF CORRECTIONS & REHABILITATION (2006) 142 CAL APP 4TH 377, 387-388, A SERIES OF EMPLOYMENT ACTIONS TAKEN TOGETHER MAY BE SUFFICIENT FOR PLAINTIFF TO PREVAIL. BY LINKING THESE ACTS TO INCREASED WORKLOADS, DOWNWARD REVIEWS AND THE FAILURE TO OBTAIN A PROMOTION, PLAINTIFF MAY BE ABLE TO ESTABLISH A CAUSAL LINK BETWEEN PROTECTED ACTIVITY AND AN ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTION. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST TO DISMISS THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS IS GRANTED. OBJECTION TO BENAY DECLARATION IS SUSTAINED BASED ON AN INABILITY TO LAY THE FOUNDATION FOR THE ADMISSIBILITY OF THE DOCUMENTS. OBJECTIONS TO THE CHOW DECLARATION A AND C THROUGH K ARE OVERRULED FOR PURPOSE OF PROVING ONLY THAT PLAINTIFF WAS COMPLAINING ABOUT THESE MATTERS. PARAGRAPH SEVEN ADMITTED FOR PLAINTIFF'S STATE OF MIND. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: BRUCE HOBAN et al VS. DANIEL FONG et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MARCH 14, 2008 LINE 5. PLAINTIFFS BRUCE HOBAN, NORMAN WHEATHLEY, LESLIE JORDAN AND MICHAEL HUDSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION PRESENTS NO EVIDENCE ON THE ISSUES OF DAMAGES. AS A CONSEQUENCE, PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF C.C.P. SECTIONS 437C(F)(1) AND 437C(P)(1). =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE ZWEMER VS. POY SIM GIN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MARCH 14, 2008 LINE 6. DEFENDANT POY SIM GIN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO DEFENDANT'S GOOD FAITH IN SERVING THE NOTICE AND WITH RESPECT TO THE INTENT TO HAVE THE SON MOVE IN. DEFENDANT ALSO FAILED TO COMPLY WITH C.C.P. SECTION 1008 IN SEEKING SUMMARY ADJUDCIATION FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR FEES IS DENIED FOR LACK OF AUTHORITY TO AWARD FEES. =(302/PJM/PB)
